questionText: A bill for $\rupee 6000$ is drawn on July 14 at 5 months. It is discounted on 5th October at 10%. Find the banker's discount, true discount, banker's gain and the money that the holder of the bill receives.

steps.explanation: Find the legally due date. Bill drawn July 14 at 5 months → nominally due December 14. Adding 3 grace days → legally due December 17.
steps.calculation: July 14 + 5 months = December 14; December 14 + 3 grace days = December 17

steps.explanation: Find unexpired time from October 5 to December 17.
steps.calculation: October: 26 days + November: 30 days + December: 17 days = 73 days = $\frac{1}{5}$ year

steps.explanation: Calculate Banker's Discount (S.I. on face value for unexpired time).
Calculate True Discount using formula $\text{T.D.} = \frac{\text{Amount} \times R \times T}{100 + R \times T}$.
Calculate Banker's Gain and money received by holder.

solution.shortcut: Unexpired time = days from discount date to legally due date; B.D. = S.I. on face value; T.D. = Amount × RT / (100 + RT).
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to add 3 grace days when computing legally due date.

questionText: If the true discount on a certain sum due 6 months hence at 15% is $\rupee 120$, what is the banker's discount on the same sum for the same time and at the same rate?

steps.explanation: Find Banker's Gain using B.G. = S.I. on T.D. for the same time and rate.
Use B.D. = T.D. + B.G. to find Banker's Discount.

solution.shortcut: B.G. = S.I. on T.D.; then B.D. = T.D. + B.G.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing B.G. as S.I. on face value instead of on T.D.

questionText: The banker's discount on $\rupee 1800$ at 12% per annum is equal to the true discount on $\rupee 1872$ for the same time at the same rate. Find the time.

steps.explanation: Since B.D. on ₹1800 = T.D. on ₹1872, the present worth of ₹1872 is ₹1800.
₹72 is S.I. on ₹1800 at 12%. Find time.

solution.shortcut: When B.D. on X = T.D. on Y, then P.W. of Y = X; difference is S.I. on X.
solution.commonMistake: Not recognising that B.D. on one sum equalling T.D. on another means the smaller sum is the P.W. of the larger.

modelName: Find time – B.D. equals T.D. on different sums

questionText: The banker's discount and the true discount on a sum of money due 8 months hence are $\rupee 120$ and $\rupee 110$ respectively. Find the sum and the rate percent.

steps.explanation: B.D. is S.I. on sum (₹1320) for 8 months at rate R.

solution.shortcut: Sum = (B.D. × T.D.) / (B.D. − T.D.); then rate from S.I. formula.
solution.commonMistake: Using T.D. instead of B.D. as the S.I. when finding rate.

modelName: Find sum and rate from B.D. and T.D.

questionText: The present worth of a bill due sometime hence is $\rupee 1100$ and the true discount on the bill is $\rupee 110$. Find the banker's discount and the banker's gain.

steps.formula: Banker's Gain from T.D. and P.W.

steps.explanation: Find Banker's Discount using B.D. = T.D. + B.G.

solution.commonMistake: Using B.G. = T.D. − P.W. instead of the correct formula.

modelName: B.D. and B.G. from P.W. and T.D.

questionText: The banker's discount on $\rupee 1650$ due a certain time hence is $\rupee 165$. Find the true discount and the banker's gain.

steps.explanation: Use the ratio: $\frac{\text{T.D.}}{\text{B.G.}} = \frac{\text{Sum}}{\text{B.D.}} - 1 = \frac{\text{Sum} - \text{B.D.}}{\text{B.D.}}$. Equivalently, $\frac{\text{Sum}}{\text{B.G.}} = \frac{\text{B.D.}}{\text{B.G.}} \Rightarrow \frac{1650}{165} = 10$, so T.D./B.G. = 10.

solution.shortcut: Sum/B.D. = T.D./B.G.; use proportionality to find T.D. then B.G. = B.D. − T.D.
solution.commonMistake: Incorrectly applying the ratio – confusing Sum with face value.

modelName: T.D. and B.G. from Sum and B.D.

questionText: What rate percent does a man get for his money when in discounting a bill due 10 months hence, he deducts 10% of the amount of the bill?

steps.explanation: Let the amount of the bill = ₹100. Money deducted = ₹10 (10% of ₹100). Money received by holder = ₹90.
The man earns ₹10 on ₹90 in 10 months. Find the rate.

solution.shortcut: Rate is on the money actually lent (face value minus deduction), not face value.
solution.commonMistake: Calculating rate on face value (₹100) instead of money advanced (₹90).

modelName: Find rate – discount deducted from bill
